L1.area: Data parsing and object construction pipeline

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the default YAML document loading interface uses a full-featured constructor capable of instantiating arbitrary Python objects, allowing attacker-controlled YAML input to achieve arbitrary code execution. A safe constructor alternative exists but is not used by default.

L3.area: YAML loading API defaults and the loader class hierarchy
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where the default YAML document loading interface uses a full-featured constructor capable of instantiating arbitrary Python objects rather than the safe constructor, allowing attacker-controlled YAML input to achieve remote code execution. The library provides both safe and unsafe loading modes, but the primary public API defaults to the unsafe mode, meaning any application that passes untrusted input to the standard loading interface is vulnerable.

ground_truth.reason: The default YAML loading API uses the full Loader class which includes Constructor, capable of instantiating arbitrary Python objects from untrusted YAML input, leading to arbitrary code execution.
